candidates.rationale: `checkApiHealth` is an exported runtime helper with no references outside or inside its own declaration site. The repo-wide `rg` search only finds the export line, so this is dead executable code rather than just an unnecessary export.
candidates.recommended_action: Delete `checkApiHealth` from `frontend/src/api.ts`; if a health probe is still intended, add a real caller before keeping the helper.

candidates.rationale: `fetchDistrictRoom` is an exported runtime helper that is never called anywhere in the repo. The only repo-wide hit is its own declaration, which makes it dead executable code.
candidates.evidence: {'code_snippet': 'export async function fetchDistrictRoom(districtId: string): Promise<RoomState | null> {\n    return apiFetch<RoomState>(`/api/districts/${encodeURIComponent(districtId)}/room`);\n}'
candidates.recommended_action: Delete `fetchDistrictRoom` from `frontend/src/api.ts`; if an initial room read is still needed, wire it into the presence flow instead of keeping an unused wrapper.

candidates.rationale: `SnapshotFreshnessCopy` is exported but never referenced outside `snapshotFreshness.ts`. It only names the local return shape for `buildSnapshotFreshnessCopy` and `useSnapshotFreshnessCopy`, so the exported type is dead public surface.
candidates.recommended_action: Remove `export` from `SnapshotFreshnessCopy`, or inline the object return type into the two local helpers if you do not want a named local interface.

candidates.rationale: `GitHubReadableRepoListResponse` is exported but never crosses a file boundary. It only appears in `api.ts` as the return type of `fetchGitHubReadableRepos`, so it is dead API surface rather than a live shared contract.
candidates.recommended_action: Remove `export` from `GitHubReadableRepoListResponse` and keep it local to `api.ts`, or inline the response shape into `fetchGitHubReadableRepos`.

candidates.rationale: `RepoRefreshStatusCopy` is exported but only used within `repoRefreshCopy.ts` to annotate the return type of `buildRepoRefreshStatusCopy`. No external file imports the interface, so the export is dead surface area.
candidates.recommended_action: Remove `export` from `RepoRefreshStatusCopy`, or inline the return type of `buildRepoRefreshStatusCopy` if you do not need a named local interface.

candidates.rationale: `SelectedRepoStatusCopy` is exported but never referenced outside `selectedRepoStatusCopy.ts`. It only names the local return shape of `buildSelectedRepoStatusCopy`, so the export is dead API surface.
candidates.recommended_action: Remove `export` from `SelectedRepoStatusCopy`, or inline the return type in `buildSelectedRepoStatusCopy` if you want to keep the shape purely local.

candidates.rationale: `GitHubTokenExchangeResponse` is exported but only used inside `api.ts` as the return and generic type for `exchangeGitHubOAuthCode`. No external file references the interface, so the exported type name is dead contract surface.
candidates.recommended_action: Remove `export` from `GitHubTokenExchangeResponse` and keep it local to `api.ts`, or inline the response shape into `exchangeGitHubOAuthCode`.

candidates.rationale: `RepoRefreshResponse` is exported but never referenced outside `api.ts`. It only annotates the local `refreshGitHubRepo` implementation, so the exported type name is dead public surface.
candidates.recommended_action: Remove `export` from `RepoRefreshResponse` and keep it local to `api.ts`, or inline the response shape inside `refreshGitHubRepo`.

candidates.rationale: `RepoRefreshNotice` is exported but only referenced inside `repoRefreshCopy.ts` as the return type of `buildRepoHudRefreshNotice`. No other file imports it, so the exported interface is dead surface area.
candidates.recommended_action: Remove `export` from `RepoRefreshNotice`, or inline the return shape of `buildRepoHudRefreshNotice` if you want to avoid a named local interface.

candidates.rationale: Heat-status classification is duplicated between the HUD and Repo City surface, and the copies have already diverged: the HUD switches to `watch` at 30 while the surface waits until 35.
candidates.recommended_action: Extract one shared heat-status helper that returns the label and reuse it from both HUD and surface rendering.

candidates.rationale: Transit path-length accumulation is implemented three times across HUD, Repo City surface, and the store, with each copy hand-rolling the same loop over path points.
candidates.recommended_action: Move path-distance calculation into one shared utility and adapt callers to a single normalized point shape.

candidates.rationale: Current-waypoint distance calculation and `m` formatting are duplicated across the 3D waypoint label, the mission objective tracker, and the HUD direction arrow.
candidates.recommended_action: Centralize waypoint-distance calculation and formatting so every mission UI surface reads from the same helper.

candidates.rationale: Selected GitHub repo status is modeled twice: `selectedRepoStatusCopy.ts` has a shared loading/error/eligible/listed-only state machine, but `MainMenu.tsx` rebuilds the same branches inline for CTA and auth-card copy.
candidates.evidence:
frontend/src/ui/MainMenu.tsx:318-357
const githubRepoActionMeta = selectedGitHubRepo
    ? selectedRepoStillIngesting
        ? connectedRepo
            ? `Waiting for GitHub ingest... ${selectedGitH
candidates.recommended_action: Promote selected-repo UI state into one shared derived model and have MainMenu, the picker, and any status cards render from that single source.

candidates.rationale: Bootstrap snapshot parsing and smoke-harness snapshot construction both manually clone and normalize repo snapshots from the same sample JSON, but they do it in separate, already-diverging code paths.
candidates.recommended_action: Extract one repo-snapshot normalization/cloning utility and have both bootstrap and smoke harness call it, with explicit options for truncation or commit override.
